mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rising rate periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, it signals a structural cooling of the labor market and elevated recession probability, which reduces expected corporate profits and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates stagflationary pressure, which compresses real consumer purchasing power and corporate margins, producing negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and falling RSAFS fails to predict negative returns, with a positive mean forward return observed in greater than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects strengthening real economic output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion periods yield negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 50% of observations, or the predictive IC drops below zero during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor market expansion and aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth periods consistently produce negative 20-day forward returns, or the signal exhibits a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation exceeds 0.8%, it forces central banks to accelerate monetary tightening, raising borrowing costs and discount rates, which systematically depresses equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally during high inflation months, with 60-day forward returns positive in greater than 65% of threshold crossings, invalidating the tightening transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with declining housing starts signal tightening credit conditions and reduced household leverage capacity, which transmits to broader corporate financing constraints and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of high FEDFUNDS and falling HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with positive forward returns observed in greater than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly real GDP growth confirms macroeconomic expansion and supports corporate earnings trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as markets price in sustained revenue growth.
failure_condition: GDPC1 expansion quarters are followed by negative 60-day equity returns in greater than 50% of instances, or the predictive relationship reverses during expansion phases.
